All right guys, time for your daily motivation slash skills training. Day number two, week three of certification. For those of you who are on it, if you're not on certification, what are you waiting for? Call up. If you have problems with money, payment, people that were working things out with scholarships and extensions, don't let this stop you, okay? Here we go. Ready? One of the things that frustrated me when I went out there into the world of self-development, right, was this whole idea of NLP, neuro-linguistic program. Many of you probably know what NLP is. Those of you who don't, all right, and you're not on certification, NLP is neuro-linguistic program. It's basically invented by a guy named Dr. Richard Bandler, a very good friend of mine, right? Love the guy and it's brilliant stuff, NLP. In some way, NLP is like staying the obvious wrapped up in science, but that's important still, just by simply taking things that are happening naturally and putting procedures to them so people can actually duplicate success. Well, that's an important thing, right? The problem I have with NLP though is there's so many charlatans out there who are practicing it who are so-called certified, don't know the first thing about NLP. See, there's one thing to get at this cost-tiles of certification as a salesperson is a different certification after this for trainers. Like there's a different level you have to get to to be a trainer. Before you go on and promote something, you really got to know it at a much deeper level. Being able to teach someone to sell or teach someone to manage their state is very different than doing it yourself. Some people can do both great, some struggle with some people with great coaches, not great salespeople. You've got both types, right? The point is, is that what bothers me about NLP is it's so powerful when it's done right, but so seldom done right. Like, go to Baylor, yeah, wow, you get some really amazing results. A lot of people out there, pure nonsense because they just get filtered down. When I learned state management from NLP, right, well for me it was sort of, A, not a big deal because I don't have an issue with managing my state. I am that oddball type. I can always get into a peak state of certainty for sales and that's very different for life in general. In business, my state never gets in my way. And there are some people like me, but most people are. Most people struggle with showing up in a state of certainty, positiveness, clarity, right, courage, not me. So when I was deciding to teach the straight line system, I knew how crucial it would be to help people manage their state. So I tried to use NLP to formalize what I had been doing back in Stratton, back in the day and all the other sales forces that I'd run and managed since then. Meaning that I knew intuitively how crucial it was to be in a peak state. You had to be in a state of certainty or else you have nothing to transfer, right? And the system that NLP use was anchoring and it just wasn't working for me. When I was using it, I found it to be really not effective, marginally at best, right? And I kept struggling with trying to find new ways to amp up NLP anchoring and ultimately I hit on this system called olfactory anchoring, which was essentially standing on the shoulder of a genius, Dr. Bandler, and adding something else onto it that really made it usable in the business world. So that's what this whole module is really about. Now it's spread out over many, many lessons. Why? Because there's building blocks, there's underpinnings, there's concepts you have to understand, psychology that rests behind it, you know, to use it more effectively. But in the end of the day, it's about olfactory anchoring, learning how to use a sense of smell to actually trick your brain or not just to, you know, get your brain to associate back into your most productive states. See clarity, comfort, courage, right? Now we're going to go through the specifics later on. This is in the later modules. Right? I just want you to understand the, it just, you know, just intellectually the importance of this. All right? Remember, the things that matter when it comes to setting a real anchor is that you have to get yourself to that absolute tippy top of the state. You have to. Without that, you're spinning your wheels. You can't set an anchor. You can't recreate a state of certainty at an eight or nine and set an anchor. You've got to be at an absolute 10 on the scale. That's the first thing. And the second drawback of NLP anchoring, again, as I explained the modules in that module, was that the anchor itself has to really stand out. It can't be just, you know, yes or clap. And that's what you see mostly at these self-development seminars. Tony's great at it. Tony doesn't bend it at anybody, but again, it doesn't really work in terms of setting as many things he does that do work Tony. But in this particular case, I'm, I don't believe that you can set an anchor like that. And I'm 99.99% sure. Because I've seen it. It's just mostly self-delusion. That one specific aspect of, of self-development. You can do it. The only way to set an anchor is to wait until you are in that state organically. So I want you to just realize this. Now, why would I make you in certification go through NLP anchoring? It's not really a powerful one that you can really recall on. But by getting you sort of close to that level and then we stack on olfactory anchoring on top of that, triggering a sense of smell at that key moment in life. Just remember, just remember this. When you show up at someone's door or you pick on the phone, you have one shot to make that first impression. You've learned that in unconscious communication, but it all starts to come back together. As you see the module start to play out, you can't do the second until you really learn the third.
